seed_problem: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
mutation: input: generalize classic one-visit-per-machine job-shop to a job-shop WITH RECIRCULATION (variable-length routings, machines may be revisited or skipped within a job) and per-test skewed 'slow workstation' durations, and replace the intractable optimum in the score by a checker-computed sequential-schedule baseline B=sum of durations so scoring stays deterministic.
title: Cryo-Harness: Quantum Lab Wiring Schedule
strategies: sequential baseline: run every operation back-to-back on one timeline (makespan = sum of durations)
job-order list scheduling: process modules one at a time, appending each operation to the earliest free slot at the end of its workstation timeline

active-schedule dispatch with gap insertion: dynamically pick the next ready operation by a priority rule (ECT / SPT / LPT / most-work-remaining) and insert it into the earliest fitting gap; run several rules plus seeded random restarts and keep the best makespan
local-search refinement over critical-path operations that reschedules bottleneck operations into gaps
